response: The EU’s GDPR, effective May 2018, mandates that any organization processing EU residents’ personal data must obtain explicit consent, grant rights to access, erasure and portability, appoint Data Protection Officers for large‐scale processing, and report breaches within 72 hours. Non-compliance can incur fines of up to 4% of annual global revenue or €20 million—penalties that have seen Meta hit with a €1.2 billion fine in 2023, Amazon €746 million, and Google over €200 million in total. GDPR has inspired similar frameworks worldwide, from California’s CCPA to Brazil’s LGPD and India’s Digital Personal Data Protection Act. Despite criticisms over burdens on small businesses, cookie-consent fatigue, and uneven enforcement across member states, the regulation has enshrined data privacy as a fundamental right and set the global standard.
